title: Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates
subtitle: This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.
caption:
Primary, secondary and tertiary education enrollment and completion rates

This is given as the gross rate, which includes children of any age entering the level of education; this can result in percentages greater than 100 because children may enter education late or repeat a year.

The line chart tracks global education metrics from 1970 to 2024, plotting five distinct series against a vertical axis ranging from 0% to over 100%. Primary enrollment, represented by the blue line, occupies the highest position throughout the timeline, fluctuating between 90% and 105% while maintaining a generally flat trajectory after the year 2000. Below this, the green line for primary completion begins near 78% in 2000 and rises steadily to approximately 88% by 2024. The lower section of the chart features a convergence of three series: secondary enrollment (orange), lower secondary completion (purple), and tertiary enrollment (brown). Secondary enrollment and lower secondary completion both start near 60% in 2000 and climb in parallel to reach roughly 78% by the end of the period. Tertiary enrollment begins at the lowest point, around 10% in 1970, and follows a continuous upward curve to finish just above 40% in 2024. The data shows that while primary participation remains near saturation, post-primary metrics display consistent growth over the observed decades. The most distinct visible pattern is the steady, uninterrupted rise in tertiary enrollment alongside the clustering of secondary-level metrics in the middle range of the scale.
